--text-3:#888; } *{box-sizing:border-box;margin:0;padding:0;} body{font-family:'DM Sans',sans-serif;color:var(--text);background:#F7F6F3;padding:32px;line-height:1.6;} .doc{max-width:840px;margin:0 auto;} .kicker{font-family:'Syne',sans-serif;font-size:12px;font-weight:700;letter-spacing:.08em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--purple);margin-bottom:8px;} h1{font-family:'Syne',sans-serif;font-size:30px;font-weight:800;letter-spacing:-.02em;margin-bottom:6px;} .sub{font-size:15px;color:var(--text-3);margin-bottom:28px;} .meta-row{display:flex;gap:24px;flex-wrap:wrap;background:#fff;border:1.5px solid rgba(0,0,0,.1);border-radius:14px;padding:16px 20px;margin-bottom:28px;} .meta-row body{background:#fff;padding:0;} .step,.meta-row,.backup,.key{box-shadow:none;} } INFO7375 · Assignment 4 · Demo Walkthrough Accessibility Monitor v2 — Live Demo Script A 4-minute walkthrough: from a manual trigger to a human-readable, AI-written audit report. Built with n8n + Claude claude-sonnet-4-6 + axe-core 4.10. Presenter Kanishk Singh Duration ~4 minutes Format Screen recording / live click-through Demo domain example.com Walkthrough — 5 steps 1 The problem, in one sentence 0:00–0:30 Say "Web teams discover accessibility regressions about 18 days late — usually when a user complains or a lawsuit lands. 96% of sites have WCAG failures. This workflow catches them weekly and, more importantly, tells a developer exactly how to fix each one in plain English." Show The Executive Summary frame of the Figma board (Part 4) — the problem/solution split and the four headline metrics ($0.026/domain, 11.8s, $78/mo vs Siteimprove's $500–$3,000/mo). 2 Trigger the workflow in n8n 0:30–1:15 Say "Here's the v2 workflow — 14 nodes, up from 8 in Assignment 3. I'll hit the manual trigger; in production a Schedule Trigger runs it every Sunday at 2am. Watch the data flow left to right." Show Open workflow_v2.json in n8n → click Test workflow . Point at the three reference-data nodes loading in parallel (axe-core rules, WCAG criteria), then the Crawl + Audit node lighting up. Call out the Set Scan Config node — domain + scan IDs are centralized, so the same workflow runs against any site. Call out that crawl + audit returns 8 violations across 5 pages. 3 The intelligence: diff → smart filter → Claude 1:15–2:30 Say "This is the part that makes it smart, not just an axe-core wrapper. The Diff Engine compares this scan to last week using a stable key — so it knows 7 are new, 1 is persisting, and 2 were resolved. Only the NEW critical and serious ones go to Claude — that's 5 calls, not 8 — which is the cost-control routing. Claude returns a structured fix for each: plain-English explanation, who's affected, the exact code change, and an effort estimate." Show Click the Diff Engine node output (7 new / 1 persisting / 2 resolved, score 62/100 vs 70). Then the Claude AI node — open one output item and read the JSON: the color-contrast fix returns the exact replacement color #B5720A and new ratio 5.2:1 . Emphasize: "It computes the replacement color and verifies the ratio — that's reasoning, not reformatting." Point at the Parse + Merge node's try/catch — malformed JSON falls back to a rule-based suggestion instead of crashing. 4 The output a human actually uses 2:30–3:30 Say "The whole point of Assignment 4 — this isn't JSON, it's something a PM can read. Here's the generated HTML report: a compliance score, every violation as a card with the AI fix, and an executive summary that traces 4 of the 7 issues to a single PricingCard component. The same report goes out as a SendGrid email, and a CSV drops into Jira." Show Open outputs/accessibility_report_example.com_2026-05-23.html in a browser. Scroll the violation cards. Then open outputs/email_digest_example.com_2026-05-23.html and outputs/compliance_trends_dashboard.html . 5 Scale + honest limits 3:30–4:00 Say "I stress-tested it across 50 domains in one reproducible run — 232 violations, 130 Claude calls after the smart filter, 50 reports rendered, zero fatal errors. Three network timeouts all auto-recovered via retry and backoff. It's projected to break near 100 domains — n8n's heap dies around 380 in-memory items — and the fix is a Split-in-Batches node. The whole path to 1,000 domains/day runs about $13.50/day. Anyone can rerun it with one command: node scale_test_50.js." Show The 50-domain dashboard ( outputs/screenshots/scale_test_50_dashboard.png ), the Scale Testing table (Part 3), and outputs/pipeline_error_log.txt showing the recovered timeouts. Key points to land The three things to make sure they hear Real intelligence, not an API call: Claude computes specific replacement colors + contrast ratios and traces multiple violations to one root-cause component. Cost-control by design: the diff engine + smart filter mean we only pay for NEW critical/serious issues — 5 calls for 8 violations, and persisting issues are never re-billed. Honest engineering: I know exactly where it breaks (heap at ~380 items) and exactly how to fix it. That's a production mindset, not a demo. Built with n8n + Claude claude-sonnet-4-6 + axe-core 4.10 Backup plan if the live demo fails If n8n won't run or the API is down Pre-generated outputs: the entire outputs/ folder is committed — walk through those files directly instead of a live run. Reproduce offline: run node generate_outputs.js — it replays captured Claude responses and regenerates every report with no API key needed. Screenshots: the Output Gallery (Part 2) and architecture diagram (Part 4) on the Figma board cover every step visually. Flagship artifact: open sample_output_audit_report.html — the full hand-reviewed

Affected element .popular-badge ✦ Claude AI Fix Suggestion Change the badge background from #F5A623 to #B5720A. New contrast vs white text = 5.2:1, which passes WCAG AA. No text or layout change needed. ⏱ 2 minutes · Risk: Low critical Ensures every form element has a label ● NEW Rule label · WCAG 1.3.1 · https://copperfox.studio/contact What's wrong The newsletter email field has only placeholder text and no real label, so screen reader users don't know what to type and the label vanishes once they start typing. Who is affected Affects screen reader users and anyone using voice control to fill the form. Affected element #newsletter-email-input ✦ Claude AI Fix Suggestion Add a real label tied to the input: before the . Preferred over aria-label here because a visible label also helps cognitive-load and voice-control users. ⏱ 3 minutes · Risk: Low serious Ensures buttons have discernible text ● NEW Rule button-name · WCAG 4.1.2 · https://copperfox.studio/blog/post-1 What's wrong The blog share button contains only an SVG icon, so assistive tech announces it as 'button' with no indication of what it does. Who is affected Affects screen reader and voice-control users who can't tell what the button does. Affected element .blog-share-btn ✦ Claude AI Fix Suggestion Add an accessible name: .
